mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window despite rate declines.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor conditions and reduced consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in greater than 55 percent of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer demand recovery, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in both series fails to produce positive 60-day returns in greater than 60 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during late-cycle expansions reflects robust manufacturing output and capacity utilization, driving positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production growth coincides with negative 20-day returns in greater than 50 percent of late-cycle observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index values erode real corporate margins and prompt anticipatory monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window despite rising inflation readings.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly nonfarm payrolls increase exceeding 150000 signals strong economic momentum and rising corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in greater than 65 percent of instances where the payroll threshold is breached.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales expansion confirm broad-based demand recovery, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in greater than 55 percent of triggered quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rates above 4.0 percent during tightening cycles increase discount rates and constrain liquidity, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in greater than 60 percent of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0 percent.
